site.title: The Story Psychology Machine
site.description: An interactive thought-tool about storytelling psychology, curiosity gaps, and trust-building narrative signal.

hero.headline: Great stories earn attention before they earn agreement.
hero.subhead: A compact lab for testing cliffhangers, curiosity gaps, and narrative trust signals in short-form writing.

premise.headline: Why this exists
paragraphs: Most writing loses people in the first two lines, not because the idea is weak, but because the opening gives no reason to continue.
Story psychology is about structured curiosity: enough tension to pull attention, enough clarity to keep trust.
This lab helps you compare weak and strong hooks, then tune the signals that make readers stay.

principles.headline: Signals that hold attention without clickbait

items.title: Curiosity Gap
items.body: Open a loop the reader wants closed, without hiding the core topic.

items.title: Narrative Tension
items.body: Show a real conflict, not artificial hype.

items.title: Payoff Promise
items.body: Signal what the reader gets if they keep going.

items.title: Credibility
items.body: Ground the hook in lived experience or observable truth.

closing.headline: Hooks are not manipulation.
closing.body: Used well, they are respect for attention: clear tension, real stakes, and a meaningful payoff.
